Notice Of Motion And Motion In Support Of Motion To Compel Deposition Of Person Most Knowledgeable Of Defendant And Cross-Complainant Confluent Medical Technologies, Inc.; Memorandum Of Points And Authories; Declaration Of Kirk B. Freeman; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Dec-24-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Kirk B. Freeman, Esq. of KBF Law, T:415.398.1082, for plaintiff/moving party;
Thomas J. Martin, Esq. of WILSON SONSINI GOODRICH & ROSATI, T:650.320.4902, for defendant/opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFFS ENDOVENTION, INC., PETER YORKE, SHAUN SAMUELS' Motion To Compel Deposition Of Person Most Knowledgeable Of Defendant And Cross-Complainant Confluent Medical Technologies, Inc., modified as follows;

Plaintiff Endovention, Inc.'s motion to compel deposition of person most knowledgeable of Defendant and Cross-Complainant Confluent Medical Technologies, Inc. is granted in part and denied in part. Both parties' requests for sanctions are denied.

Plaintiff's motion is granted as to topic (1) ("a detailing of how many EMC catheters Confluent Medical Technologies, Inc. built during the period of the 1802.b contract, details of how many of those EMC catheters were built for pre-Design Verification testing, Design Verification testing and First-in-Human testing, the current location of each of those EMC catheters and details as to the disposition of those EMC catheters no longer in the possession, custody or control of Confluent Medical Technologies, Inc."). The topic in question is directly relevant to Plaintiff's claims for breach of contract and breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. In light of the narrow focus of the topic, it should not be burdensome for Defendant to designate a person most knowledgeable to testify to it.

Plaintiff's motion is denied as to topic (2) ("The identity of clients who stopped doing business with Confluent Medical Technologies, Inc. in 2018 and the reasons why.").) This topic is at best tangentially relevant to Plaintiff's fraud-based claims in the seventh and eighth causes of action, which are based on representations that Defendants allegedly made in 2017. (Compl. 89, 97.) Further, Defendant shows that Plaintiff's unpled theory of liability is contradicted by the record.

The deposition shall be limited to one hour.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
